general.prompt: What's causing more than 1,200 people to experience 'explosive diarrhea'?
options: A new spicy chip challenge gone wrong
A bad batch of kombucha from a trendy Brooklyn café
A parasite outbreak
A reusable rocket crash that poisoned the water supply
general.summary: The CDC is investigating a cyclosporiasis outbreak linked to contaminated food or water, with nearly 1,000 cases in Michigan.

general.prompt: Which company just raised $26.5 billion in a record-breaking US IPO?
options: SK Hynix
A new Ariana Grande-themed cryptocurrency
A Chinese rocket company that just recovered its booster
A potato chip manufacturer trying to go public
general.summary: South Korea's SK Hynix made the largest-ever US IPO by a foreign firm, surpassing Alibaba's 2014 debut.

general.prompt: Why did Ariana Grande look-alike Paige Niemann feel 'misled'?
options: By a fake alien abduction story on TikTok
By a CDC warning about exploding toilets
By a promise of free concert tickets that never came
By a DM from Ariana Grande
general.summary: Paige Niemann said she felt misled by a direct message Ariana Grande sent her years ago amid her rise to fame.

general.prompt: What do migrants who witnessed a fatal shooting by ICE in Houston say happened?
options: The victim was actually a government spy from North Korea
The victim was trying to escape a giant parasitic worm
The victim had just been to a spicy chip contest
The victim did not ram officers
general.summary: Three men in the vehicle with Lorenzo Salgado Araujo say he did not ram officers, contradicting DHS's account.

general.prompt: What did China recover after the maiden launch of the Long March 10B rocket?
options: A lost air fryer from space
A record-breaking IPO prospectus from SK Hynix
A parachute containing leftover kimchi
The reusable first stage of the rocket
general.summary: China recovered the reusable first stage of the Long March 10B rocket after a vertical landing on a sea platform.

tech.prompt: What brought thousands of Pokémon Go players to Times Square like a swarm of Pikachu on caffeine?
options: To protest the game's lag issues
To test Samsung's new foldable phone features
To catch a giant inflatable Pikachu
To defeat Mewtwo
tech.summary: A surprise 10th-anniversary event saw Niantic fulfilling a promise teased in the original 2016 launch trailer for Pokémon Go.

tech.prompt: What's about to cost more than a virtual hot dog at a digital race track?
options: The price of a racing wheel controller
In-game fuel for virtual cars
iRacing subscription costs
GameSpot employee salaries
tech.summary: iRacing will increase the cost of all its subscription plans this coming August.

tech.prompt: Which gaming website got hit with more layoffs than a Chansey uses Soft-Boiled?
options: IGN
Polygon
Kotaku
GameSpot
tech.summary: GameSpot experienced its fifth round of layoffs since being purchased by Fandom.

tech.prompt: What phone is allegedly stealing camera features from another phone like a Team Rocket heist?
options: Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra
Galaxy Note 20
iPhone 16 Pro Max
Galaxy S26 Ultra
tech.summary: Samsung’s Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra is expected to offer a 200MP primary rear camera, borrowing from the Galaxy S26 Ultra.

tech.prompt: What data from your wrist gizmo do doctors actually want you to care about?
options: Your sleep score to improve gaming performance
Your step count to beat Mewtwo
Heart and health metrics that actually help you live longer
Your calorie burn to match iRacing subscription costs
tech.summary: Health trackers collect a ton of data, but doctors recommend focusing on specific metrics for better health outcomes.

business.prompt: Who just raised a record-breaking $26.5 billion in a US IPO and made Alibaba look like chump change?
options: Samsung (they just wanted attention too)
SoftBank (they were busy soaring 11% in Asia tech stocks)
SK Hynix
A new chip startup called 'Silicon Unicorn'
business.summary: South Korea's SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US IPO by a foreign firm, surpassing Alibaba's 2014 debut.

business.prompt: After a chip-led rally, what did stock futures do? Hint: They didn't party as hard as SoftBank!
options: They did the hokey pokey and turned themselves around
They moonwalked into a new bull market
They rallied even higher thanks to discount gas stations
They dipped
business.summary: Stock futures dipped after a chip-led rally, while SoftBank soared 11% as Asia tech stocks rose.

business.prompt: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh just tapped a Trump ally to advise on AI reshaping work. Who's the lucky venture capitalist?
options: Elon Musk (because he needs another side gig)
Fidji Simo (fresh off stepping down from OpenAI)
A sentient AI named 'Advisor-9000'
Marc Andreessen
business.summary: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh named venture capitalist Marc Andreessen to co-lead a task force on AI's economic impact.

business.prompt: Who stepped down from leading OpenAI's AGI work due to illness, moving to a part-time advisor role?
options: Fidji Simo
Marc Andreessen (too busy advising the Fed)
A ChatGPT hallucination that thought it was human
Sam Altman (he just wanted to focus on his meme stock portfolio)
business.summary: OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as AGI chief and transitioning to part-time advisor due to illness.

business.prompt: Trump-promoted discount gas stations are fueling a mystery. What's the big question mark?
options: Questions about the gas stations
If you can pay with crypto and get a free MAGA hat
How they're related to SK Hynix's IPO
Whether they also sell invisible fuel
business.summary: Discount gas stations promoted by Donald Trump are fueling a mystery and raising questions.

sports.prompt: Why did Kawhi Leonard's trade to the Raptors hit a speed bump? Did he order a poutine with too much cheese?
options: The deal was stalled, but details remain unclear
The Raptors couldn't find a good parking spot for their dinosaur mascot
LA Galaxy coach says Team USA was 'too naive'
Kawhi demanded a lifetime supply of maple syrup
sports.summary: Kawhi Leonard's trade to the Raptors was paused, as reported by Yahoo Sports.

sports.prompt: What happened when Conor McGregor and Max Holloway faced off? Did they exchange smoothie recipes?
options: They had a tense, unfriendly stare-down
Unbeaten Spain meets star-powered Belgium in the World Cup quarterfinals
They hugged and sang ‘Kumbaya’
They arm-wrestled for a bag of potatoes
sports.summary: Conor McGregor and Max Holloway had a heated face-off ahead of their UFC rematch.

sports.prompt: Who stole the show in the Wizards vs. Jazz Summer League game? Was it a dancing bear?
options: A bear in a Wizards jersey dunked from the free-throw line
The halftime entertainer won MVP
AJ Dybantsa made an immediate statement
Conor McGregor and Max Holloway have heated face-off
sports.summary: AJ Dybantsa led the Wizards to a 92-88 win over Darryn Peterson's Jazz in his Summer League debut.

sports.prompt: How many goals has Spain conceded in the World Cup? Are they playing with a force field?
options: LA Galaxy coach says Team USA was 'too naive'
Their goalkeeper is actually a hologram
They've let in 10 goals but keep winning anyway
Zero goals conceded
sports.summary: Spain is unbeaten and hasn't conceded a goal at the World Cup, with a 36-match unbeaten streak.

sports.prompt: Why did LA Galaxy coach Greg Vanney say Christian Pulisic struggled? Did he forget to eat his Wheaties?
options: He accidentally wore his lucky socks inside out
An early injury disrupted his rhythm
He was distracted by a shiny object on the field
Kawhi deal paused & Summer League takeaways
sports.summary: Vanney said Pulisic's early injury robbed him of momentum in a tough World Cup knockout loss to Belgium.

science.prompt: What shiny treasure did scientists find hiding on the seafloor, making everyone wonder if we should start underwater mining?
options: A sunken treasure chest full of pirate coins
A giant pearl the size of a beach ball
Invisible gold
A trillion-sun quasar
science.summary: Japan discovered a potential underwater gold mine with record-breaking concentrations of invisible gold on the seafloor.

science.prompt: What did brave scientists sample from inside Hawaii's most active volcano?
options: Tephra from the crater wall
Invisible gold from the seafloor
Lava-flavored ice cream
Volcanic dust bunnies
science.summary: Scientists from the U.S. Geological Survey flew into Kilauea's summit to collect tephra samples from the crater wall.

science.prompt: What did astronomers discover that shines with the light of a trillion suns?
options: A disco ball in space
The oldest quasar ever seen
A supernova that never goes out
A leap hour for timekeepers
science.summary: Scientists discovered the oldest quasar ever seen, which shines with the light of a trillion suns.

science.prompt: What new underwater habitat is now operational for aquanauts to live in?
options: An invisible gold mine
A submerged research station with bunk beds
A giant clam shell converted into a hotel
An underwater human habitat
science.summary: A U.S. underwater human habitat, part laboratory and part dormitory, is now operational for aquanauts to live on the seafloor.

science.prompt: What new timekeeping unit are officials considering to replace leap seconds?
options: A tephra sample
A leap hour
A leap millennium
A leap minute
science.summary: Timekeepers are considering a leap hour as an alternative to the problematic leap second.
